codeReview.summary: The diff improves helper-level coverage for realtime audio aliases and session-payload parsing, but it does not fully close the runtime parity gap it claims. Websocket/SIP transport semantics are still effectively placeholder behavior, and GA-style output voice/speed settings are dropped when building session payloads.

issues.description: The new websocket/SIP transport normalization is still dead helper logic instead of runtime behavior. `OpenAIRealtimeWebSocketModel::connect()` only flips `connected = true` (`openai_realtime.rs:203-205`), never consuming `connection_url()` (`openai_realtime.rs:57-95`) or `transport.api_key`, and `update_session()` only caches `last_session_payload` (`openai_realtime.rs:246-250`) without applying it anywhere. `OpenAIRealtimeSIPModel::connect()` likewise only checks for `call_id` and then flips the same boolean (`openai_realtime.rs:264-271`), so different call IDs are behaviorally indistinguishable and no actual SIP attachment semantics are preserved. That falls short of VAL-RT-003's requirement that websocket/SIP models preserve connection/config semantics. The upstream implementations wire these values into the real connection path (`reference/openai-agents-js/packages/agents-realtime/src/openaiRealtimeWebsocket.ts:354-375`; `reference/openai-agents-python/tests/realtime/test_openai_realtime_sip_model.py:30-45`).

`session_payload_from_settings()` drops GA-style output audio settings during session updates. The config surface already supports nested `audio.output.voice` and `audio.output.speed` (`crates/agents-realtime/src/config.rs:47-50`), but the payload builder only serializes `settings.voice` and never includes `speed` (`openai_realtime.rs:183-189`). Callers that provide the newer GA-style nested output config therefore lose their requested voice/speed when `update_session()` builds the payload, which breaks the session-payload normalization/application semantics this feature was supposed to close. Upstream prefers nested output-audio voice/speed before falling back to deprecated top-level fields (`reference/openai-agents-python/src/agents/realtime/openai_realtime.py:1418-1440`).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The runtime-parity-worker procedure could be more explicit that realtime transport parity must be proven with runtime-wiring assertions, not only helper/unit tests. This feature added unit coverage inside `crates/agents-realtime/src/openai_realtime.rs` for `connection_url`, payload parsing, and the `call_id` guard, but no new facade/runtime test asserts that connect/update_session actually consume those values, which allowed dead transport-helper code to look complete.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Commit d0ffa4c only changed `crates/agents-realtime/src/audio_formats.rs`, `crates/agents-realtime/src/openai_realtime.rs`, and `crates/agents-realtime/src/runner.rs`; the transcript skeleton for worker session `1866c60c-a537-458a-b08e-72308bf9a291` shows targeted `agents-realtime --lib` runs plus existing `realtime_semantics` / `extensions_semantics`, but no new cross-crate assertion covering custom websocket URLs, applied session payloads, or SIP call attachment behavior.

summary: Fail. The feature added helper-level normalization and alias coverage, but the realtime runtime still does not apply the new websocket/SIP transport semantics at connect/update time, and GA-style output voice/speed settings are dropped from session payloads.
